title: Time Management Tools for Startups (Complete Guide)
author: Caylin White

sections.heading: Key Takeaways
sections.text: Time management tools help small businesses eliminate repetitive tasks, giving teams more time to focus on strategic work.A CRM can act as a central hub, connecting different departments and activities to ensure alignment and reduce duplicate work.When choosing a tool, consider an intuitive interface, the ability to grow with your business, and the capability to integrate with existing systems like email and calendars. In business, the difference between surviving and thriving often comes down to your choices and how you manage your time. When you’re juggling roles as CEO, sales rep, marketer, and customer support all at once, even the most dedicatedsmall and medium-sized business (SMB)owners can be stretched too thin. The right time management tools can transform chaos into clarity, eliminating energy-draining repetitive tasks and giving you back hours in your week. These digital solutions aren’t just nice-to-haves — they’re the foundation that enables your business to scale without burning out your team. Let’s explore what makes time management essential for your business growth and what tools can help. What we’ll cover: What are time management tools?How time management tools impact the entire businessTypes of time management toolsKey features to look for in a time management toolChoosing tools that work for smaller teamsThe role of a CRM in managing time and productivityHow AI is changing time management for SMBsMake every minute count: Finding your time management solution

sections.heading: What are time management tools?
sections.text: Time management toolsare software systems designed to help individuals and teams plan, track, and optimize their work by organizing their time. From calendar apps and task trackers to AI-powered workflow tools, these tools are designed to reduce stress, improve focus, and prevent important things from slipping through the cracks. Rather than simply working more hours,effective time managementhelps you make better use of the time you already have, allowing you to accomplish more without sacrificing your wellbeing.

sections.heading: How time management tools impact the entire business
sections.text: While personal productivity matters, the real power of time management tools emerges when implemented across your entire operation. These tools change how your whole business functions. According to Salesforce’s Small and Medium Business Trends Report,66% of growing SMBs say technology is critical to their ability to stay efficient and competitive. Using a unified time management system helps teams stay aligned, avoid duplicate work, and communicate more clearly. It also sets clear responsibilities, leading to smoother service and happier customers. When your team manages time well, customers notice. Orders arrive on time, questions get prompt responses, and promises are kept‌ — ‌creating the kind of reliable experience that builds customer loyalty.

Pro Tip:All of these can be found in acustomer relationship management (CRM)tool.

sections.text: When evaluating time management tools for your small business, consider these core features: Ease of use: Look forintuitive toolsthat require minimal training. Complex interfaces only create additional time burdens for you and your team.Scalability:As your business grows, your tools should grow with you. sections.heading: Choosing tools that work for smaller teams
sections.text: One of the biggest time management mistakes small businesses make is using disconnected tools that don’t communicate with each other. When your calendar doesn’t talk to your task list, which doesn’t connect to your customer database, you waste precious time switching between applications and manually transferring information. Consider this scenario: Asmall ecommerce businessuses SalesforceStarter Suite, which you’ve trained to integrate their customer information, team calendar, and task management. When a customer places a large order, the system automatically creates follow-up tasks for thefulfillmentteam, schedules a check-in call for the account manager, and sets reminders for marketing to send a satisfaction survey‌ — ‌all without anyone having to manually coordinate these actions.

sections.heading: The role of a CRM in managing time and productivity
sections.text: While many think of a CRM as just a sales tool, they now serve as comprehensive time management hubs that support every department in your business. A CRM becomes the central nervous system of your operation, connecting: Salesactivities (managing leads, scheduling follow-ups)Marketing campaigns(coordinating launches, measuring engagement)Customer service(managing cases, monitoring response times)Operations (tracking orders, managinginventory) A CRM likeStarter Suitehelps small businesses stay organized and build stronger customer relationships. You can assign tasks directly from customer records, making sure nothing gets missed. And now,artificial intelligence (AI)handles routine emails and reminders for you, so you can stay connected without the manual work. And with all your upcoming activities in one place, it’s easier to plan your day and focus on what matters most. For businesses ready for the next level,Pro Suitebuilds on these capabilities with AI-powered suggestions that help you focus on the most promising opportunities first. With AI-powered agents likeAgentforce 360, you get multiple capabilities for automating your marketing, sales, and service. More time and more efficiency to help evensolo foundersoperate like a full-fledged team.

This boost in efficiency helped increase sales by 25% and shorten accounts receivable cycles by 15 days, resulting in a 942% ROI.

raw_html:
Key Takeaways

  • Time management tools help small businesses eliminate repetitive tasks, giving teams more time to focus on strategic work.
  • A CRM can act as a central hub, connecting different departments and activities to ensure alignment and reduce duplicate work.
  • When choosing a tool, consider an intuitive interface, the ability to grow with your business, and the capability to integrate with existing systems like email and calendars.
This summary was created with AI and reviewed by an editor.

In business, the difference between surviving and thriving often comes down to your choices and how you manage your time. When you’re juggling roles as CEO, sales rep, marketer, and customer support all at once, even the most dedicated small and medium-sized business (SMB) owners can be stretched too thin. 

The right time management tools can transform chaos into clarity, eliminating energy-draining repetitive tasks and giving you back hours in your week. These digital solutions aren’t just nice-to-haves — they’re the foundation that enables your business to scale without burning out your team. Let’s explore what makes time management essential for your business growth and what tools can help.

What we’ll cover:

  • What are time management tools?
  • How time management tools impact the entire business
  • Types of time management tools
  • Key features to look for in a time management tool
  • Choosing tools that work for smaller teams
  • The role of a CRM in managing time and productivity
  • How AI is changing time management for SMBs
  • Make every minute count: Finding your time management solution

What are time management tools?

Time management tools are software systems designed to help individuals and teams plan, track, and optimize their work by organizing their time. From calendar apps and task trackers to AI-powered workflow tools, these tools are designed to reduce stress, improve focus, and prevent important things from slipping through the cracks.

Rather than simply working more hours, effective time management helps you make better use of the time you already have, allowing you to accomplish more without sacrificing your wellbeing.

Boost team productivity with Salesforce

Boost Productivity With Slack For Small Business

Top 27 Small Business Tools To Try Today

The Biggest Business Secret – Slack

9 Slack Hacks For Startups and Small Businesses

How time management tools impact the entire business

While personal productivity matters, the real power of time management tools emerges when implemented across your entire operation. These tools change how your whole business functions. According to Salesforce’s Small and Medium Business Trends Report, 66% of growing SMBs say technology is critical to their ability to stay efficient and competitive. 

Using a unified time management system helps teams stay aligned, avoid duplicate work, and communicate more clearly. It also sets clear responsibilities, leading to smoother service and happier customers. When your team manages time well, customers notice. Orders arrive on time, questions get prompt responses, and promises are kept‌ — ‌creating the kind of reliable experience that builds customer loyalty.

Trailhead: Personal Productivity

Learn how to stay productive while working from home with Trailhead, Salesforce’s free online learning platform.

Visit Trail
+300 points
Trail

Personal Productivity

Types of time management tools

There are a few different time management solutions to meet different needs — here are a few to get started:

  • Time trackers: Monitor how you and your team spend working hours to identify inefficiencies and opportunities.
  • Digital planners: Help you map out days, weeks, and months with to-do lists and priorities.
  • Calendar integrations: Coordinate meetings and block time for focused work to prevent scheduling conflicts.
  • Workflow automation: Handle repetitive tasks so you can focus on higher-value activities that grow your business.
  • Task management: Track projects from inception to completion with clear ownership and deadlines.

Pro Tip: All of these can be found in a customer relationship management (CRM) tool.

Key features to look for in a time management tool

When evaluating time management tools for your small business, consider these core features:

  • Ease of use: Look for intuitive tools that require minimal training. Complex interfaces only create additional time burdens for you and your team.
  • Scalability: As your business grows, your tools should grow with you. Look for solutions that can handle more users, projects, and complexity without needing a complete overhaul.
  • Real-time collaboration: Especially important in today’s hybrid work environments, tools like Slack allow team members to coordinate seamlessly regardless of location.
  • Integration capabilities: Choose tools that connect with your existing systems like email, calendars, and most importantly, your CRM.
  • Reporting and analytics: The ability to measure time spent on different activities helps identify bottlenecks and opportunities for improvement in your processes.
  • Automation features: The best time management tools include automation to eliminate repetitive tasks and simplify workflows, freeing up valuable time.

Choosing tools that work for smaller teams

One of the biggest time management mistakes small businesses make is using disconnected tools that don’t communicate with each other. When your calendar doesn’t talk to your task list, which doesn’t connect to your customer database, you waste precious time switching between applications and manually transferring information.

Consider this scenario: A small ecommerce business uses Salesforce Starter Suite, which you’ve trained to integrate their customer information, team calendar, and task management. When a customer places a large order, the system automatically creates follow-up tasks for the fulfillment team, schedules a check-in call for the account manager, and sets reminders for marketing to send a satisfaction survey‌ — ‌all without anyone having to manually coordinate these actions.

Set up your digital storefront, engage customers, and sell more using a commerce-ready platform with integrated tools for every sale.

Check it out

The role of a CRM in managing time and productivity

While many think of a CRM as just a sales tool, they now serve as comprehensive time management hubs that support every department in your business. A CRM becomes the central nervous system of your operation, connecting:

  • Sales activities (managing leads, scheduling follow-ups)
  • Marketing campaigns (coordinating launches, measuring engagement)
  • Customer service (managing cases, monitoring response times)
  • Operations (tracking orders, managing inventory)

A CRM like Starter Suite helps small businesses stay organized and build stronger customer relationships. You can assign tasks directly from customer records, making sure nothing gets missed.

And now, artificial intelligence (AI) handles routine emails and reminders for you, so you can stay connected without the manual work. And with all your upcoming activities in one place, it’s easier to plan your day and focus on what matters most.

For businesses ready for the next level, Pro Suite builds on these capabilities with AI-powered suggestions that help you focus on the most promising opportunities first. With AI-powered agents like Agentforce 360, you get multiple capabilities for automating your marketing, sales, and service. More time and more efficiency to help even solo founders operate like a full-fledged team.

Wherever you are — just get started.

No matter where you are on your journey as a business owner, you can get started with Starter Suite for free — the CRM made for growth.

Try it for free

How AI is changing time management for SMBs

Remember how AI was once available only to large enterprises with big budgets? Today, Salesforce is making AI accessible to small and growing businesses through tools like Agentforce 360, which acts like a time-saving digital assistant.

These AI capabilities transform time management by:

  • Suggesting the next best actions based on customer data and past interactions
  • Automatically capturing and organizing information from emails and calls
  • Identifying which leads or customers need attention most urgently
  • Pre-filling forms and data entry tasks that typically consume hours each week

A real-world example: ICS+, a small automation control business with just six employees, used to juggle manual processes and multiple tools, which slowed them down. By switching to Salesforce, they centralized all project and client info, improving collaboration and cutting internal emails by 20%. This boost in efficiency helped increase sales by 25% and shorten accounts receivable cycles by 15 days, resulting in a 942% ROI.
